From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Light-welterweight](/w/index.php?title=Light-welterweight&redirect=no "Light-welterweight"))

Weight class in boxing and kickboxing

**Light welterweight**, also known as **junior welterweight**[[1]](#cite_note-1) or **super lightweight**,[[2]](#cite_note-2) is a [weight class](/wiki/Weight_class "Weight class") in [combat sports](/wiki/Combat_sport "Combat sport").

## Boxing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Light_welterweight&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Boxing")]

### Professional boxing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Light_welterweight&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Professional boxing")]

In [professional boxing](/wiki/Professional_boxing "Professional boxing"), light welterweight is contested between the [lightweight](/wiki/Lightweight "Lightweight") and [welterweight](/wiki/Welterweight "Welterweight") divisions, in which boxers weigh above 61.2kg or 135 pounds and up to 63.5 kg or 140 Ibs. The first champion of this weight class was [Pinky Mitchell](/wiki/Pinky_Mitchell "Pinky Mitchell") in 1922, though he was only awarded his championship by a vote of the readers of the *Boxing Blade* magazine.

There was not widespread acceptance of this new weight division in its early years, and the [New York State Athletic Commission](/wiki/New_York_State_Athletic_Commission "New York State Athletic Commission") withdrew recognition of it in 1930. The [National Boxing Association](/wiki/National_Boxing_Association "National Boxing Association") continued to recognize it until its champion, [Barney Ross](/wiki/Barney_Ross "Barney Ross") relinquished the title in 1935 to concentrate on regaining the [welterweight](/wiki/Welterweight "Welterweight") championship.

A few commissions recognized bouts in the 1940s as being for the light welterweight title, but the modern beginnings of this championship date from 1959 when [Carlos Ortiz](/wiki/Carlos_Ortiz_(boxer) "Carlos Ortiz (boxer)") won the vacant title with a victory over [Kenny Lane](/wiki/Kenny_Lane "Kenny Lane"). Both the World Boxing Association (WBA) and the World Boxing Council (WBC) recognized the same champions until 1967, when the WBC stripped [Paul Fuji](/wiki/Paul_Fuji "Paul Fuji") of the title and matched [Pedro Adigue](/wiki/Pedro_Adigue "Pedro Adigue") and Adolph Pruitt for their version of the championship. Adigue won a fifteen-round decision. The [International Boxing Federation](/wiki/International_Boxing_Federation "International Boxing Federation") (IBF) recognized [Aaron Pryor](/wiki/Aaron_Pryor "Aaron Pryor") as its first champion in 1984. [Hector Camacho](/wiki/Hector_Camacho "Hector Camacho") became the first [World Boxing Organization](/wiki/World_Boxing_Organization "World Boxing Organization") (WBO) champion with his victory against [Ray Mancini](/wiki/Ray_Mancini "Ray Mancini") in 1989.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

### Amateur boxing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Light_welterweight&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Amateur boxing")]

In [amateur boxing](/wiki/Amateur_boxing "Amateur boxing"), light welterweight is a weight class for fighters weighing up to 64 [kilograms](/wiki/Kilograms "Kilograms"). For the [1952 Summer Olympics](/wiki/1952_Summer_Olympics "1952 Summer Olympics"), the division was created when the span from 54 to 67 kg was changed from three weight classes ([featherweight](/wiki/Featherweight "Featherweight"), [lightweight](/wiki/Lightweight "Lightweight"), and [welterweight](/wiki/Welterweight "Welterweight")) to four. Perhaps the most famous amateur light welterweight champion is [Sugar Ray Leonard](/wiki/Sugar_Ray_Leonard "Sugar Ray Leonard"), who went on to an impressive professional career.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

* [2012](/wiki/Boxing_at_the_2012_Summer_Olympics "Boxing at the 2012 Summer Olympics") – ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/58/Flag_of_Cuba_%283-2%29.svg/40px-Flag_of_Cuba_%283-2%29.svg.png) [Roniel Iglesias](/wiki/Roniel_Iglesias "Roniel Iglesias") ([CUB](/wiki/Cuba_at_the_2012_Summer_Olympics "Cuba at the 2012 Summer Olympics"))
* [2016](/wiki/Boxing_at_the_2016_Summer_Olympics "Boxing at the 2016 Summer Olympics") – ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/00/Flag_of_Uzbekistan_%283-2%29.svg/40px-Flag_of_Uzbekistan_%283-2%29.svg.png) [Fazliddin Gaibnazarov](/wiki/Fazliddin_Gaibnazarov "Fazliddin Gaibnazarov") ([UZB](/wiki/Uzbekistan_at_the_2016_Summer_Olympics "Uzbekistan at the 2016 Summer Olympics"))

## Kickboxing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Light_welterweight&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Kickboxing")]

* In the [International Kickboxing Federation](/wiki/International_Kickboxing_Federation "International Kickboxing Federation") (IKF), Super lightweight is 132.1 - 137 lbs or 60.04 - 62.27 kg, & Light welterweight is 137.1 - 142 lbs or 62.31 - 64.54 kg.

## Lethwei

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Light_welterweight&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Lethwei")]

The [World Lethwei Championship](/wiki/World_Lethwei_Championship "World Lethwei Championship") recognizes the light welterweight division with an upper limit of 63.5 kg (140 lb). In [World Lethwei Championship](/wiki/World_Lethwei_Championship "World Lethwei Championship") [Antonio Faria](/w/index.php?title=Antonio_Faria&action=edit&redlink=1 "Antonio Faria (page does not exist)") is the Light welterweight Champion.
